title: Lemon Thyme Chicken with Orzo & Roasted Asparagus
description: Home Chef staff pick — pan-seared chicken thighs with a bright lemon-thyme pan sauce, served over creamy parmesan orzo with roasted asparagus. Light, elegant, and packed with fresh flavor.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Trim asparagus, toss with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper. Spread on a baking sheet.
Pat chicken thighs dry. Season generously with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Add chicken skin-side down; cook 5–6 minutes undisturbed until skin is deep golden and crispy. Flip and cook 2 more minutes.
Transfer skillet to oven. Roast 18–20 minutes until chicken reaches 165°F. Add asparagus to oven for the last 12 minutes.
Remove chicken to a plate. Return skillet to medium heat. Add minced shallots and garlic; cook 2 minutes. Add white wine and scrape up browned bits.
Add 2 cups chicken broth and bring to a simmer. Add orzo and thyme sprigs. Cook, stirring frequently, 8–10 minutes until orzo is tender and most liquid is absorbed.
Remove thyme sprigs. Stir in butter, parmesan, lemon zest, and juice of 1 lemon. Season to taste.
Serve chicken over creamy parmesan orzo with roasted asparagus. Drizzle with pan juices and garnish with lemon slices and fresh thyme.
tips: Starting the chicken skin-side down in a cold-ish pan (not screaming hot) gives the fat time to render and the skin to get extra crispy.
Bone-in thighs have much more flavor than boneless — the bones add richness to the pan sauce.
Keep stirring the orzo as it cooks — it can stick to the bottom of the pan.
For a lighter version, use boneless skinless thighs and reduce cooking time by 5 minutes.
